transcripts.title: V09 — Readable Objects
transcripts.description: Verify ReadableTrait on plaques, brochure, and warning sign

commands.output:
Family Zoo
By Sharpee Tutorial

Type HELP for instructions.

Taken.
commands.comment: Take and read the zoo brochure (portable + readable)

commands.output:
Zoo brochure reads:
WILLOWBROOK FAMILY ZOO — Your Guide

EXHIBITS:
  Petting Zoo ............ East from Main Path
  Aviary ................. West from Main Path
  Nocturnal Animals ...... Staff Area (ask a keeper!)

FEEDING TIMES:
  Goats & Rabbits ........ 10am, 2pm
  Birds .................. 11am, 3pm

FREE ACTIVITIES:
  Souvenir penny press ... Main Path
  Scavenger hunt ......... Pick up a card at the entrance!

"Where every visit is a wild adventure!"
commands.comment: V9 teaches: ReadableTrait — read vs examine

commands.output: A glossy tri-fold brochure with "WILLOWBROOK FAMILY ZOO" on the cover in cheerful yellow letters. A cartoon elephant waves from the corner.
commands.comment: Examine shows physical description, not text

commands.output:
Main Path

A wide gravel path winds through the heart of the zoo. Colorful direction signs point every which way. A park bench sits beside the path. To the east, the petting zoo. To the west, the aviary. A staff gate blocks the path to the south. The entrance is back to the north.

You can see staff gate, direction signs, flower beds, souvenir penny, park bench, lunchbox here.
commands.comment: Go to Petting Zoo and read the plaque

commands.output:
Petting Zoo

A cheerful open-air enclosure filled with friendly animals. Pygmy goats trot around nibbling at visitors' shoelaces, while a pair of fluffy rabbits hop lazily near a hay bale. A feed dispenser is mounted on a post near the entrance. An info plaque is posted by the gate. The main path is back to the west.

You can see pygmy goats, hay bale, rabbits, info plaque, bag of animal feed, feed dispenser here.

Info plaque reads:
PYGMY GOATS — These Nigerian Dwarf goats are gentle, curious, and always hungry. They can eat up to 3% of their body weight daily. Please use only zoo-approved feed from the dispensers.

HOLLAND LOP RABBITS — Known for their floppy ears and calm temperament. Our pair, Biscuit and Marmalade, were born right here at Willowbrook in 2023.

Main Path

A wide gravel path winds through the heart of the zoo. Colorful direction signs point every which way. A park bench sits beside the path. To the east, the petting zoo. To the west, the aviary. A staff gate blocks the path to the south. The entrance is back to the north.

You can see staff gate, direction signs, flower beds, souvenir penny, park bench, lunchbox here.
commands.comment: Go to Aviary and read that plaque

commands.output:
Aviary

You step inside a soaring mesh dome. Brilliantly colored parrots chatter from rope perches, and a toucan eyes you curiously from a branch overhead. A small waterfall splashes into a stone basin. An info plaque hangs near the entrance. The exit back to the main path is to the east.

You can see toucan, parrots, waterfall, rope perches, aviary plaque here.

Aviary plaque reads:
WELCOME TO THE AVIARY — Home to over 30 species of tropical and temperate birds!

TOCO TOUCAN (Ramphastos toco) — The largest toucan species. Despite its enormous bill, it weighs less than a smartphone.

SCARLET MACAW (Ara macao) — Can live over 75 years. Our oldest macaw, Captain, is 42 and has outlasted three zookeepers.

Main Path

A wide gravel path winds through the heart of the zoo. Colorful direction signs point every which way. A park bench sits beside the path. To the east, the petting zoo. To the west, the aviary. A staff gate blocks the path to the south. The entrance is back to the north.

You can see staff gate, direction signs, flower beds, souvenir penny, park bench, lunchbox here.
commands.comment: Navigate to supply room for the warning sign

commands.output:
Zoo Entrance

You stand before the wrought-iron gates of the Willowbrook Family Zoo. A cheerful welcome sign arches over the entrance, and a small ticket booth sits to one side. A sturdy iron fence runs along either side of the gates. The main path leads south into the zoo grounds.

You can see staff keycard, welcome sign, ticket booth, iron fence, zoo map, backpack here.

Main Path

A wide gravel path winds through the heart of the zoo. Colorful direction signs point every which way. A park bench sits beside the path. To the east, the petting zoo. To the west, the aviary. A staff gate blocks the path to the south. The entrance is back to the north.

You can see staff gate, direction signs, flower beds, souvenir penny, park bench, lunchbox here.
You unlock staff gate with staff keycard.

Supply Room

A cluttered storage room behind the staff gate. Metal shelves line the walls. A cork board on the wall is covered with staff schedules. The staff gate leads back north.

You can see metal shelves, cork board, warning sign, flashlight here.

Warning sign reads:
CAUTION: The Nocturnal Animals Exhibit is kept dark to simulate the animals' natural nighttime environment. Please use a flashlight if available. Do NOT use camera flash — it distresses the animals. Report any escaped animals to staff immediately. (Yes, it has happened. We don't talk about the Great Owl Incident of 2022.)
